cards.def: To encourage or assist someone to do something wrong, in particular, to commit a crime.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Aid and abet a criminal"
cards.context: Often paired with negative actions to indicate collaboration or secondary compliance in wrongdoing.
cards.vis: A shady man places 'a bet' with a criminal, encouraging him to rob a bank so he can win the wager.

cards.def: Easy to approach, reach, enter, speak with, or understand.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Make literature accessible to the public"
cards.context: Used by ETS to describe dense text that is surprisingly clear, or public venues available to all.
cards.vis: A giant neon ramp that makes a sky-high castle easily 'able' to be 'accessed' by toddlers.

cards.def: Excessively interested in acquiring money or material things.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An acquisitive corporate society"
cards.context: Appears in prompts critiquing consumerism, greed, or materialistic modern behaviors.
cards.vis: An octopus with vacuum cleaners for tentacles frantically trying to 'acquire' every gold coin on the ocean floor.

cards.def: To combine or unite to form one organization or structure.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Amalgamate two distinct companies into one corporate giant"
cards.context: Frequently used in passages about corporate mergers, cultural assimilation, or mixing distinct elements.
cards.mnem: A-malgam (like a dental amalgam filling mixing metals).
cards.vis: A mad scientist throws gold, silver, and copper into a blender to create a singular giant metallic monster.

cards.def: To reduce the force, effect, value, or thickness of something.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Attenuate the effects of the virus"
cards.context: Appears in scientific or political contexts where an original force or signal is weakened over time or distance.
cards.mnem: At ten, you ate less, making you thin.
cards.vis: A giant booming speaker is muffled by a tiny blanket until the sound wave becomes a microscopic whisper.

cards.def: To make something greater by adding to it; increase.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Augment income with a second job"
cards.context: Used when resources, capabilities, or physical sizes are supplemented to enhance overall capacity.
cards.vis: A small plant suddenly explodes into a massive tree during the month of August.

cards.def: A strong dislike or disinclination toward something.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An aversion to public speaking"
cards.context: Appears in psychological or behavioral texts describing a subject avoiding specific stimuli.
cards.vis: A man recoils in absolute horror when offered a plate containing a slimy, moving version of an alien organism.

cards.def: Showing a casual and cheerful indifference considered to be callous or improper.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A blithe disregard for the rules"
cards.context: ETS loves the dual nature of this word: ostensibly joyful, but contextually signaling an irresponsible lack of concern.
cards.vis: A person cheerfully gliding on roller skates through a crowded, chaotic car crash scene without a care in the world.

cards.def: The feeling that a person or a thing is beneath consideration, worthless, or deserving scorn.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Look at someone with utter contempt"
cards.context: Used to describe adversarial relationships, critical reviews, or a total lack of respect for authority.
cards.mnem: Con-tempt (Scorn those who attempt to con you).
cards.vis: A king sneering down from his high throne, throwing garbage at a peasant who tries to bow before him.

cards.def: To waste time; to be slow.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Don't dawdle on your way to school"
cards.context: Contrasted against efficiency, promptness, or urgency in sentence completion questions.
cards.vis: A snail wearing a tiny watch stops to draw pictures in the mud while an emergency rescue mission passes by.

cards.def: To cause something to change direction by interposing something; turn aside from a straight course.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Deflect criticism onto others"
cards.context: Can be physical (shields) or psychological (shifting blame/attention in interviews).
cards.vis: A politician using a literal steel mirror to bounce incoming critical questions back at the reporters.

cards.def: To regard a possibility, fact, or person as being unworthy of consideration or inherently false.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Discount the rumors as mere gossip"
cards.context: In GRE questions, it rarely means a price reduction; it almost always means to dismiss or undervalue an argument.
cards.vis: A scientist takes a pile of data sheets and aggressively throws them directly into a trash can because they don't match his theory.

cards.def: A person who opposes official policy, especially that of an authoritarian state.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A political dissident speaking out against the regime"
cards.context: Used in historical or political contexts alongside themes of rebellion, censorship, and orthodoxy.
cards.vis: A lone protestor standing in front of a massive army of identical clones, holding up a bright red 'NO' sign.

cards.def: Successful in producing a desired or intended result; effective.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An efficacious medical treatment"
cards.context: Commonly tests scientific, medical, or administrative efficacy in problem-solving scenarios.
cards.vis: A single drop of a magic potion completely curing a sick dragon instantly.

cards.def: Fair and impartial.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An equitable distribution of resources"
cards.context: Appears in legal, social justice, or economic contexts stressing impartiality over favoritism.
cards.vis: A judge measuring out slices of a cake using a high-precision laser scale to ensure everyone gets the exact same millimeter.

cards.def: Not even or regular in pattern or movement; unpredictable.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Erratic behavior that alarms neighbors"
cards.context: Contrasted with stability, consistency, and predictable trends.
cards.vis: A car steering wheel spinning wildly on its own, charting a zig-zag course all over a highway.

cards.def: Diligent and hard-working.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An industrious student preparing for exams"
cards.context: Paired with productivity, focus, and long hours of focused labor.
cards.vis: A beaver wearing a hardhat building a fifteen-story wooden skyscraper all by itself overnight.

cards.def: To give character or essence to; to be the characteristic quality of; animate or inspire.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Religious beliefs that inform his poetry"
cards.context: GRE specific usage: Not just giving data, but serving as the underlying core philosophy or structural foundation of something.
cards.vis: A glowing blue matrix code flowing into a clay statue, bringing it to life and shaping its every movement.

cards.def: Irritating; annoying.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An irksome habit of interrupting people"
cards.context: Describes minor but persistent annoyances that wear down patience over time.
cards.vis: A tiny mosquito buzzing right inside someone's ear while they try to play a high-stakes game of Jenga.

cards.def: To shackle; restrain someone with handcuffs or chains.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Manacled by archaic rules"
cards.context: Used literally for prisoners or metaphorically to describe institutions restricting freedom/innovation.
cards.vis: Heavy iron chains locking a runner's ankles directly to the track right before a race starts.

cards.def: Unassuming or moderate in the estimation of one's abilities; relatively moderate, limited, or small.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A modest house with simple furniture"
cards.context: Can denote humble character, or refer to an amount that is unexceptional or limited in scope.
cards.vis: A multi-billionaire choosing to drive a rusty, old bicycle to a gala event instead of a limousine.

cards.def: Harmful, poisonous, or very unpleasant.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Noxious fumes escaping from the factory"
cards.context: Often used for literal toxins, or metaphorically for toxic ideologies or corrupting environments.
cards.vis: A bright green, bubbling cloud of gas that wilts every flower it touches instantly.

cards.def: Having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "The pernicious influence of fake news"
cards.context: Differentiated from overt harm; pernicious implies a slow, creeping, insidious danger.
cards.vis: A beautiful, golden apple that slowly rots from the inside out via a hidden worm.

cards.def: A difficult, unpleasant, or embarrassing situation.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Find oneself in a financial predicament"
cards.context: Used when describing characters facing structural dilemmas with no easy way out.
cards.vis: A man hanging upside down from a tree branch by his shoelaces while an alligator waits directly underneath.

cards.def: Competent or skilled in doing or using something.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Proficient in several programming languages"
cards.context: Used to denote high skill levels or technical fluency in specialized tasks.
cards.vis: A master chef slicing onions blindfolded at lightning speed without making a single mistake.

cards.def: Using or containing too many words; tediously lengthy.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A prolix text that bores readers to sleep"
cards.context: Appears in critiques of overly academic, dense, or unnecessarily wordy prose.
cards.vis: A speaker pulling a endless scroll out of his pocket that unrolls down the street while he continues talking.

cards.def: The feeling or belief that someone or something is worthless or despicable; contempt.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Pour scorn on a ridiculous proposal"
cards.context: Shows strong disapproval or dismissal of theories, trends, or behaviors.
cards.vis: An art critic tearing a canvas in half while laughing mockingly at the artist.

cards.def: Lower in rank or position; subservient or secondary.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Subordinate personal desires to the common good"
cards.context: Can be a noun (lower employee) or verb (to make something less important than another priority).
cards.vis: A small soldier kneeling down to hand his sword up to a giant general.

cards.def: Not proper or appropriate; unbecoming.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An unseemly argument at a funeral"
cards.context: Tests societal expectations or protocols being broken by gauche, rude, or scandalous behavior.
cards.vis: A guest arriving at a black-tie royal wedding wearing a clown suit and neon flippers.

cards.def: Used as an intensifier, often to qualify a metaphor; real, genuine, or absolute.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A veritable gold mine of information"
cards.context: Used to stress that a metaphorical description is shockingly true or comprehensive.
cards.vis: A treasure chest that glows with verified, real magic light when opened by an explorer.
